summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg View article about personality tests in hiring. The full argument about how personality tests fail American workers by filtering out candidates for wrong reasons is contained in the linked Bloomberg piece, not in this blog post itself. The post serves as a pointer to her external journalism work.
key_points: Personality tests used in hiring often filter out candidates for illegitimate reasons
These tests are failing American workers rather than serving them
O'Neil's critique is part of her broader Bloomberg View commentary on algorithmic and data-driven systems
money_quotes: All too often, they filter people out for the wrong reasons.
key_insight: Personality tests in hiring processes serve as gatekeepers that exclude qualified workers based on flawed criteria.
